negative_variants.description: Slew Rate DAC4: forces the output to zero, so it compiles and idles plausibly but fails every nonzero code check.
Slew Rate DAC4: decodes the binary input correctly but jumps directly to the target instead of enforcing the slew-rate limit.
Slew Rate DAC4: uses code divided by 16, passing monotonic smoke checks while missing the full-scale endpoint calibration.
Slew Rate DAC4: treats d0 as the MSB and d3 as the LSB, so symmetric code smoke tests can pass but bit-order coverage fails.
Slew Rate DAC4: reaches the right endpoints eventually but slews at half the required rate, failing ramp-shape timing checks.
